title: Smoky Flaky Saudia Tomato Stir-fry
description: A lazy-sunday dish featuring baby plum tomatoes slow-cooked with a smoky flavor profile, perfect for pescatarian diets.
instructions: Preheat your oven to 350°F (175°C). Rinse and drain the white beans.
Melt 2 tablespoons of olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Add minced garlic, red chili flakes, salt, and black pepper. Sauté for about 1 minute until fragrant.
Add baby plum tomatoes to the skillet with the sautéed garlic mixture. Mix well.
Fold in the white beans and sprinkle dried oregano on top. Stir gently to combine all ingredients evenly.
Transfer the stir-fry to a baking dish and bake for about 15-20 minutes until the tomato slices are tender and the mixture is slightly crispy at the edges.
Serve hot with crusty bread or as a side dish.
